data.title: Hut 8 buys 990 bitcoin
data.description:
• Hut 8 (NASDAQ:HUT) said it has bought ~990 bitcoin (BTC-USD).
  • The firm said the aggregate purchase price of the acquired BTC was ~$100 million, or an average of ~$101.7K apiece.
  • The company's strategic BTC reserve now stands at 10,096 BTC.

data.title: Zomedica appoints Scott Jordan as EVP, finance & CFO
data.description:
• Zomedica has appointed Scott A. Jordan as its Executive Vice President, Finance and Chief Financial Officer, effective January 1, 2025.
  • Prior to joining Zomedica, Scott Jordan was the CEO and Founder of S. Jordan Associates, a boutique investment bank headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, where Scott worked with life sciences and diagnostics companies to raise non-dilutive capital and private placements, close strategic partnerships, and facilitate listings on public stock exchanges.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fennec Pharmaceuticals partially repays its outstanding convertible debt facility
data.description:
• Fennec Pharmaceuticals (TSX:FRX:CA) has made partial repayment of $13 million of the approximately $32 million outstanding convertible debt facility with Petrichor Healthcare Capital Management. This early partial repayment was financed entirely with available cash.
  • Post repayment, the convertible debt facility with Petrichor will be approximately $19 million and maintain a maturity of September 2027.
  • The partial repayment will save approximately $1.5 million in future annual interest payments and eliminate potential dilutive shares.
  • As previously reported in Fennec’s third quarter 2024 earnings and inclusive of this announcement, the Company anticipates that its cash, cash equivalents and investment securities will be sufficient to fund planned operations into 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: American Healthcare REIT declares $0.25 dividend
data.description:
• American Healthcare REIT (NYSE:AHR) declared $0.25/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.65%
  • Payable Jan. 17; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See AHR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Lamb Weston raises dividend to $0.37/share; boosts stock buyback plan
data.description:
• Lamb Weston (NYSE:LW) declares $0.37/share quarterly dividend, 2.8% increase from prior dividend of $0.36.
  • Forward yield 1.89%
  • Payable Feb. 28; for shareholders of record Jan. 31; ex-div Jan. 31.
  • In December 2024, the Board of Directors approved an increase of $250M in the company's existing $500M share repurchase authorization under the program to an aggregate total of $750M. After giving effect to this increase, ~$558M remained authorized and available for repurchase under the share repurchase program.
  • See LW Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: X Financial to repurchase $48.7M ordinary shares from major shareholder
data.description:
• X Financial (NYSE:XYF) to repurchase 6.35M ADS, representing 38.09M Class A ordinary shares at US$7.67 per ADS for a total repurchase price of ~US$48.7M from its major shareholder.
  • The company has approved a new share $50M Class A ordinary shares repurchase plan, effective until June 30, 2026.
  • Upon completion of the repurchase from major shareholder, the Company's two previous share repurchase programs will complete, and approximately US$15.9 million will remain for future potential repurchases under the new US$50 million share repurchase plan.
  • The company returned ~US$76.0 million in value to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases, including US$16.5 million in cash dividends, US$9.2 million in the tender offer, US$48.7 million in the recent repurchase, and US$1.6 million in other repurchases.
  • The company repurchased equivalent to 52.2M CLass A ordinary shares in 2024, or 17.8% of ordinary shares issued and outstanding as of December 31, 2023.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Regis buys Alline Salon Group for $22M
data.description:
Regis (NASDAQ:RGS) has acquired Alline Salon Group (ASG), its largest franchisee, in a transaction valued at $22M of initial consideration.

Under the terms of the purchase agreement, Regis will acquire all of Alline’s issued and outstanding membership interests for $22M in a cash and stock transaction, of which $19M of the initial consideration was delivered in cash, in addition to shares of Regis common stock valued at $3M.

The agreement also includes additional performance-based earnout opportunities of up to $3M, in $1M increments, over the next three years.

The transaction is accretive to adjusted EBITDA and provides Regis a turn-key operating infrastructure, bringing it closer to salon operations alongside franchisees.

"This acquisition marks another key milestone for Regis and further positions our company for future growth. The transaction represents a path towards strong EBITDA growth from a business that we know intimately, having previously owned and operated the salons that we are acquiring. With an attractive purchase multiple, combined with further operational initiatives and identified synergies, we believe there is substantial value creation potential for Regis and our stakeholders,” Regis CEO Matthew Doctor said.

Synergies of $1M-1.5M identified will likely be achieved in calendar 2026.

data.title: Lamb Weston appoints Michael Smith as CEO
data.description:
• Lamb Weston (NYSE:LW) said it has appointed its current COO, Michael Smith, as its CEO, effective January 3.
  • Smith will succeed Thomas Werner in the role.
  • Werner will serve in an advisory role through August 31.
  • Smith had joined the firm in 2007.

data.title: Stock index futures rise a day after Fed-fueled selloff
data.description:
Stock index futures climbed on Thursday, a day after Wall Street tumbled as the Federal Reserve projected fewer rate cuts in 2025.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.53%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.33%.

U.S. stocks on Wednesday slumped after the Federal Reserve projected higher inflation and fewer rate cuts in 2025. The estimates came after the central bank announced a quarter-point rate cut, as widely expected.

"There might only be six days until Christmas, but markets still had time for another surprise yesterday, as a hawkish cut from the Fed saw the S&P 500 post its biggest decline after a Fed meeting since 2001," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

While the Fed delivered another 25 basis point rate cut on Wednesday, the overall messaging from the central bank was a much slower pace of policy easing in 2025.

"The moves led to a significant cross-asset slump, with the 10 year Treasury yield closing above 4.5% for the first time since May, whilst the VIX index of volatility surged, and touched its highest since the market turmoil back in the summer," Allen added.

The Fed cut rates as expected, but hawkish forecasts and press conference language surprised markets, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

On the economic calendar for Thursday, the third estimate of the Q3 GDP will land before the opening bell.

The initial jobless claims will also be release at the same time, and is expected to fall to 229K.

The November existing home sales and November leading indicators are also slated to land during market hours. The former is expected to rise to 4.09M, while the latter is forecasted to come in at -0.1% M/M.

The Fed Balance Sheet will be released later in the day.

data.title: Lamb Weston Non-GAAP EPS of $0.66 misses by $0.35, revenue of $1.6B misses by $70M
data.description:
• Lamb Weston press release (NYSE:LW): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.66 misses by $0.35.
  • Revenue of $1.6B (+25.0% Y/Y) misses by $70M.

  • Updated Fiscal 2025 Outlook

    • Net sales of $6.35 billion to $6.45 billion
    • GAAP net income target of $330 million to $350 million, and Diluted EPS target of $2.30 to $2.45
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $1,170 million to $1,210 million
    • Adjusted Net Income target of $440 million to $460 million and Adjusted Diluted EPS target of $3.05 to $3.20
  • In addition, the Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.37 per share of Lamb Weston common stock, a $0.01 increase. The dividend is payable on February 28, 2025 to stockholders of record as of the close of business on January 31, 2025.
  • In December 2024, the Board of Directors approved an increase of $250 million in the Company's existing $500 million share repurchase authorization under the program to an aggregate total of $750 million. After giving effect to this increase, approximately $558 million remained authorized and available for repurchase under the share repurchase program.
